content.claim: BTMN (behavioral/pedagogical needs category) prevalence in Hungary shows sharp age discontinuity: 2.1% of preschool children versus 9.5% of school-age children as of 2018/19, a fivefold difference suggesting school-entry academic/behavioral demands contribute substantially to identification
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: Data is six years old as of 2025; no more recent granular BTMN breakdown publicly available
Age discontinuity is consistent with but does not definitively prove demand-driven identification hypothesis
No within-school progression data provided to distinguish between intake-at-age-6 effect versus age-of-identification artifact
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
